title: Platner replacement risks “back room” perception, but keeping him is “way too risky”
summary: Democrats again must sub in a candidate who didn’t win a primary. This didn’t work in the 2024 presidential election. Jessica Tarlov and Brian Tyler Cohen discuss, on Raging Moderates.
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/PTB9lxNCX_o

Transcript

I am very fearful of a 2024 redux, especially voters who are very aligned with Platner politically because there are people out there who think that, right, that the Dem establishment came to get him. >> Look, a lot of these subsequent elections, you you attempt to at least fix some of the immediate problems that you contended with in the previous election. So maybe we don't nominate someone who's 80 years old. Maybe we try to make it as democratic a a process as we possibly can. So, of course, if we have a situation where suddenly we have another election where primary voters made their voices heard and then

something happens where now we're thrust into a process where somebody can be chosen, you know, look, it's not a back room, but but so to speak, people are going to perceive this as a back room. And so, yeah, of course, I'm worried the alternative to choosing somebody by one of these processes that may be viewed as less democratic is to keep in place a candidate who now has a credible rape accusation against him. Look, is it ideal? Absolutely not. But the alternative is is way too risky and so we have to just engage in this process that frankly nobody wants to engage in.

But, uh, you know, hopefully we can just suck it up and and get somebody who is dynamic and charismatic and has a progressive agenda on the ballot and, uh, and and rely on the fact that this is going to be an environment where Democrats are going to overperform and and hopefully that's enough to get us over the finish Find
